free_text: Measurement of activation cross sections of the proton
induced nuclear reactions on palladium
AVF cyclotron
CGR-560 cyclotron
High purity Pd foils, thickness: 12.14 and 7.99 micron.
Pd foils were stacked with Al and Cu monitor foils.
Incident proton energies: 70 MeV at CYRIC in Japan,
37.3, 28.5 and 15.9 MeV at VUB in Belgium.
beam intensities: 100-200 nA,
irradiation time: 0.1-1.0 hours.
The beam intensity, the incident energy and the energy
degradation were controlled by a method based on flux
constancy via normalization to the parallel measured
excitation functions of the monitor reactions.
The measurements started after a few hours after the
end of bombardment at VUB and after one and half day
at CYRIC.
The beam currents were deduced from the monitor
reactions, the number of the target nuclei from the
precise mass and surface determination of the target
foil.
The calculated energy degradation of the incident
beam was checked and corrected on the basis of the
monitor reactions.
Nondestructive measurement without chemical separation.
Chapter 4
Energy degradaton in the stacks was calculated by using
the stopping power data and polynomial approximation of
Andersen and Ziegler.
Decay data were taken from the ENSDF, NuDat data files.
For estimation of the uncertainties of the
cross sections the linearly contributing processes were
taken into account by equal sensitivities. The errors
of nonlinear parameters (time) were not considered but
their contribution was minimal compared to other
processes.The typical total resulting errors were
around 8-13%.
Calculations for the excitation functions using
the ALICE-IPPE code were performed with a view to
estimate the weight of the different contributing
reactions and to check the trend and the magnitude of
the obtained experimental data.
